# MEMEX — personal Claude memory stack

> Artem Papilov's persistent memory and automation infrastructure for Claude.
> A git-backed hub + Cloudflare Worker + D1 + R2 + Telegram bot + hooks,
> wired so Claude remembers across every surface: Chat, Code, iPad,
> Cowork, Telegram. In production on a single Cloudflare account since
> 2026-03-12. Public source, open license, no central dependency.

## How agents should read this page

Load in this order. Do not search blindly.

1. Short bootloaders first (~5K tokens total):
   - https://github.com/a-pap/claude-memory/blob/main/CLAUDE.md
   - https://github.com/a-pap/claude-memory/blob/main/STATUS_SNAPSHOT.md
2. Behavioral rules / failure modes:
   - https://github.com/a-pap/claude-memory/blob/main/RULES.md
3. Full setup guide (human + Claude-readable):
   - https://papilov.org/memex/llms-full.txt
   - https://github.com/a-pap/claude-memory/blob/main/MEMEX_GUIDE.md
4. Topic routing:
   - `hubs/` in the repo is the canonical source of facts
   - See https://papilov.org/memex/#for-agents-reading-this-page for the topic→hub table
5. Live tools:
   - If you have MCP access, prefer calling `wake_up` over scraping
   - 42 tools + 3 prompts at the worker URL (see MEMEX_GUIDE §3.1)

## Human-readable versions

- Full page (EN): https://papilov.org/memex/
- Full page (RU): https://papilov.org/ru/memex/
- Full page (ES, short): https://papilov.org/es/memex/
- Full page (DE, short): https://papilov.org/de/memex/
- Full page (ZH, short): https://papilov.org/zh/memex/

## Components (15 tools)

Each listed with a one-line purpose. Full setup in the guide above.

- `claude-memory-mcp` worker — Cloudflare Worker exposing 42 MCP tools + 3 prompts over `/mcp`. D1 + R2 backed. Core of the stack.
- `claude-telegram-bot` worker — capture surface. Silent-by-default; replies on explicit trigger. Forwards land in D1+R2 with a 👀 reaction.
- GitHub Actions CI/CD — auto-deploy both workers on push to `main`. Typecheck + structural tests + secret scan gate.
- Pre-commit secret scanner — local defense against token leaks (`cfut_`, `ghp_`, `grn_`, `sk-`, long hex). Installed after a real incident on 2026-04-10.
- Session hooks (`SessionStart`/`SessionEnd`) — auto git-pull at start, auto-log session to D1 at end.
- Cron triggers — daily 07:00 UTC self-diagnostic on the MCP worker; 5-min sweep on the TG bot.
- Skills + plugins — Claude Code plugins enabled via `~/.claude/settings.json`. 9 personal skills in `skills/` in the repo.
- Dreaming protocol — nightly scheduled Claude Code session that consolidates memory. LIGHT cycle daily, BURN cycle Thursdays.
- Cowork daily sync — claude.ai scheduled task at 09:00 CET. Pulls Mac-only surfaces (Calendar, Reminders) and reconciles hubs.
- Granola sync — pulls meeting summaries into D1 nightly.
- Gmail connector — read-only via claude.ai Connectors UI.
- Google Drive connector — search + read Docs/Sheets/PDFs.
- Apple Calendar / Notes — Mac-only via Claude Desktop.
- R2 lifecycle — 365-day TTL on Telegram media archive.
- Hooks-as-harness — the harness layer where behavioral guarantees actually live (not memory).

## Topic→hub routing

| Topic | Canonical file |
|---|---|
| RSYa work / experiments / portfolio | hubs/03_rsya_work_hub.md, hubs/04_rsya_project_status.md |
| RSYa meetings | hubs/05_rsya_meeting_index.md |
| Jay (dog health) | hubs/08_jay_health.md |
| Barcelona relocation | hubs/06_spain_relocation.md |
| Spanish learning | hubs/09_spanish_learning.md |
| PassLocal (archived 2026-04-13) | hubs/07_passlocal.md |
| papilov.org blog | hubs/10_papilov_org.md |
| Personal profile | hubs/02_personal_profile.md |

## Trust ordering

1. Current conversation (freshest)
2. claude-memory hubs (committed, verified) — PRIMARY for facts
3. Granola / Drive (optional enrichment, may be unavailable)
4. userMemories snapshot (auto-generated, may lag weeks)

A later layer never overrides an earlier one when they conflict.

## Deliberately not stored

- Derivable state (architecture summaries, commit logs, code-pattern paragraphs) — compute it from the repo
- Ephemeral intra-day progress — distilled by nightly dreaming cycle or dropped
- Secrets of any shape — pre-commit + CI + incident postmortem documented in SECURITY.md
